DECLARATION OF STEPHANIE SESSIONS PERKINS IN SUPPORT OF PETITION TO COMPEL BINDING ARBITRATION AND STAYING PROCEEDINGS FILED BY DEFENDANT LEVY, ERLANGER AND COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA BUSINESS ENTITY TYPE UNKNOWN LEVY, DAVID AN INDIVIDUAL ERLANGER, WILLIAM

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS LEVY, ERLANGER AND COMPANY, DAVID LEVY AND WILLIAM ERLANGER'S PETITION TO COMPEL BINDING ARBITRATION. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY LITIGATION IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1281.2. PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS FORMED A VALID ARBITRATION AGREEMENT AND THE AGREEMENT COVERS PLAINTIFF'S DISPUTE WITH DEFENDANTS. THE AGREEMENT IS NOT UNCONSCIONABLE: (1) PLAINTIFF OFFERS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE DEFENDANTS PRESENTED THE AGREEMENT TO THE PLAINTIFF ON A TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT BASIS OR THAT SIGNING THE AGREEMENT WAS A CONDITION OF EMPLOYMENT; (2) THE LANGUAGE OF THE AGREEMENT IS STRAIGHTFORWARD; (3) THE ARBITRATION PROVISION WAS ON THE 3RD PAGE OF A 4 PAGE DOCUMENT AND THE WORD "ARBITRATION" IS IN BOLD; AND (4) THE AAA RULES INCORPORATED INTO THE AGREEMENT ARE FAIR. DEFENDANTS WERE NOT REQUIRED TO PROVIDE A COPY OF THE AAA RULES WITH THE AGREEMENT. THE ARBITRATION PROVISION IN THE AGREEMENT COVERS PLAINTIFF'S DISPUTE WITH THE DEFENDANTS; ALL OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARISE FROM HER EMPLOYMENT WITH THE DEFENDANTS AND THE AGREEMENT DEFINED THE SCOPE OF PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT. DEFENDANTS ATTEMPTED TO NEGOTIATE A MUTUALLY AGREEABLE ARBITRATOR WITH THE PLAINTIFF, BUT THE PLAINTIFF DEMANDED THE DEFENDANTS TO AGREE ONLY TO PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED ARBITRATORS BEFORE FILING THE COMPLAINT. FINALLY, DEFENDANTS ARE THE PROPER PARTIES TO THE ARBITRATION PROVISION BECAUSE LEVY & COMPANY IS PRESENTLY KNOWN AS LEVY, ERLANGER AND COMPANY AND PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF "WAS HIRED BY DEFENDANT LEVY AND COMPANY (PRESENTLY LEVY, ERLANGER AND COMPANY) . . . ." (ERLANGER DECL. 1:24-25; COMPLAINT 14.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAY-28-2014 CONTINUED TO FEB-25-2015 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR STATUS OF ARBITRATION STAY. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-12-2014 Continued To Feb-25-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition To Compel Binding Arb
Off Calendar Apr-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LAUREN S. KADISH OF THE CHAPMAN, GLUCKSMAN & BARGER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (310) 207-7722 EXT. 284 BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS LEVY, ERLANGER AND COMPANY, DAVID LEVY and WILLIAM ERLANGER's Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration. Defendants' motion to compel arbitration and stay litigation is granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.2. Plaintiff and Defendants formed a valid arbitration agreement and the agreement covers Plaintiff's dispute with Defendants. The Agreement is not unconscionable: (1) Plaintiff offers no evidence that the Defendants presented the Agreement to the Plaintiff on a take it or leave it basis or that signing the Agreement was a condition of employment; (2) the language of the Agreement is straightforward; (3) the arbitration provision was on the 3rd page of a 4 page document and the word "arbitration" is in bold; and (4) the AAA rules incorporated into the Agreement are fair. Defendants were not required to provide a copy of the AAA rules with the Agreement. The arbitration provision in the Agreement covers Plaintiff's dispute with the Defendants; all of Plaintiff's claims arise from her employment with the Defendants and the Agreement defined the scope of Plaintiff's employment. Defendants attempted to negotiate a mutually agreeable arbitrator with the Plaintiff, but the Plaintiff demanded the Defendants to agree only to Plaintiff's proposed arbitrators before filing the Complaint. Finally, Defendants are the proper parties to the arbitration provision because Levy & Company is presently known as Levy, Erlanger and Company and Plaintiff's Complaint alleges that Plaintiff "was hired by Defendant Levy and Company (presently Levy, Erlanger and Company) . . . ." (Erlanger decl. 1:24-25; Complaint 14.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976
